title: Add t_dpi_open_query to test DPI array query functions
body:
I have added this while looking into #2293. It passes on the big 3, but Verialtor throws an internal error in V3Combine:
```
./t/t_dpi_open_query.pl --vlt
==SUMMARY: Left 1  Passed 0  Failed 0  Unsup 0  Time 0:00
======================================================================
vlt/t_dpi_open_query: ==================================================
        perl /home/gl/work/verilator/src/bin/verilator --prefix Vt_dpi_open_query ../obj_vlt/t_dpi_open_query/Vt_dpi_open_query__main.cpp --exe --make gmake --x-assign unique -cc -Mdir obj_vlt/t_dpi_open_query -OD --debug-check --comp-limit-members 10 -Wall -Wno-DECLFILENAME --clk clk  -f input.vc +define+TEST_OBJ_DIR=obj_vlt/t_dpi_open_query t/t_dpi_open_query.cpp t/t_dpi_open_query.v    > obj_vlt/t_dpi_open_query/vlt_compile.log
%Error: Internal Error: t/t_dpi_open_query.v:166:18: ../V3Combine.cpp:97: Call list broken, points to call w/different func
  166 |          do if ((cSvSize1(e1, d)) !== (2*d+1)) begin $write("%%Error: %s:%0d:  got='h%x exp='h%x\n", "t/t_dpi_open_query.v",166, (cSvSize1(e1, d)), (2*d+1)); $stop; end while(0);
      |                  ^~~~~~~~
                        ... See the manual and https://verilator.org for more assistance.
%Warning: vlt/t_dpi_open_query: Exec of perl failed: %Error: Internal Error: t/t_dpi_open_query.v:166:18: ../V3Combine.cpp:97: Call list broken, points to call w/different func

vlt/t_dpi_open_query: %Error: Exec of perl failed: %Error: Internal Error: t/t_dpi_open_query.v:166:18: ../V3Combine.cpp:97: Call list broken, points to call w/different func
vlt/t_dpi_open_query: FAILED: Exec of perl failed: %Error: Internal Error: t/t_dpi_open_query.v:166:18: ../V3Combine.cpp:97: Call list broken, points to call w/different func
==SUMMARY: Passed 0  Failed 1  Unsup 0  Time 0:01
```

It disappears when you add --debug to the above, so it might be some uninitialized variable thing but Valgrind is severely unhappy with a lot of other issues. I haven't tried address sanitizer. I haven't looked at V3Combine before so it might take me a while to figure this out.

@wsnyder if you have some time could you take a look at the inernal error? I am happy to get test working afterwards.
